claim: `restorePane` calls `restorePaneModel` (the pure reducer) and receives a workspace with both `restored.layout` and `restored.minimized`, but then discards `restored.minimized` and recomputes the filtered minimized list independently inside the `set()` closure: `(st.minimized[wsId] ?? []).filter(id => id !== paneId)`. The two computations produce identical results today, but they can silently diverge if `restorePaneModel` is extended with additional pruning. The plan (TASK-003) intended the pure reducer's output to be applied atomically; using only half of it creates an implicit, unenforced contract between the reducer and the store action.
recommendation: Use `restored.minimized` from the reducer's return value to update `minimized[wsId]` in the `set()` call, dropping the redundant `filter` expression. Since `restorePaneModel` receives `{ ...ws, minimized: ids }`, `restored.minimized` equals `ids.filter(id => id !== paneId)` today â€” swapping is a safe, zero-behavior-change refactor that removes the divergence risk.
resolution: resolved (TASK-020): restorePane uses restored.minimized.

claim: REQ-013 states: 'The minimize command's display in the toolbar/context-menu tooltip and any native-menu accelerator MUST derive from the keybinding registry (formatChord over the resolved toggle-minimize-pane binding), never a hard-coded duplicate.' The context-menu item renders `<button>Minimize</button>` with neither a `title` tooltip nor a visible chord label. `PaneToolbar.tsx:25-26` correctly derives the toolbar tooltip from `formatChord`, satisfying REQ-013 there, but the context-menu surface â€” specifically named in the MUST â€” carries no chord at all. A user who discovers the feature only through the right-click menu cannot learn the keybinding from that entry point, and the display will not update if the binding is rebound.
recommendation: Add a `title` attribute to the minimize button in `PaneContextMenu` constructed via `resolveBindings` + `formatChord` over `toggle-minimize-pane`, mirroring the pattern in `PaneToolbar.tsx:25-26`. The store's `quick.keybindings` is already accessible at the call site (it is read in the parent for similar purposes). Alternatively, append the chord in parentheses to the button label text.
resolution: re-review CONFIRMED-CLOSED: context-menu chord title registry-derived (TASK-017); TEST-044 green.

claim: `toggleMinimize` and `toggleMaximize` both update only the runtime store maps (`st.minimized`, `st.maximized`) and never write `ws.minimized` / `ws.maximized` on the workspace record. The workspace record's view-state fields are therefore chronically stale in memory between save/load cycles. All current callers that need the live state correctly override `ws.minimized` before passing to pure reducers (`{ ...ws, minimized: ids }` in `restorePane`; `{ ...ws, minimized: minimizedIds }` in `WorkspaceView`). But this convention is implicit and not type-enforced â€” any future feature that reads `ws.minimized` directly will silently see stale data. Additionally, `minimizePane` in `workspace-model.ts` was written to update `ws.minimized` and was intended (per TASK-003) to be called by `toggleMinimize`, but is never called by it, leaving it as dead call-site code relative to its designed role in the store.
recommendation: Document the split-state contract in a prominent comment (or a named helper type) so that workspace records are understood to carry stale view-state in memory and live view-state must always be read from the runtime store maps. Consider marking `minimizePane` in `workspace-model.ts` as 'for pure-reducer unit tests only' in its doc-comment, or un-exporting it if tests access it through a barrel, to prevent future store actions from incorrectly calling it and expecting `ws.minimized` to be authoritative.
resolution: resolved (TASK-020): split-state doc-comment; minimizePane marked pure-reducer/test surface.

claim: The `normalizeViewState` doc-comment at line 194 states 'Empty view-state is represented by ABSENT fields â€¦ so a workspace with no minimized/maximized panes round-trips byte-identical.' However, `applyViewState` (internals.ts:36) unconditionally writes `minimized: minimized ?? []` and `maximized: maximized ?? null`. When no pane is minimized or maximized, `minimized[id]` is `undefined`, so the workspace file is always written with `"minimized": []` and `"maximized": null`. On the next load, `normalizeViewState` strips those empty/null fields. The actual round-trip for a clean workspace is: Write â†’ `{"minimized":[],"maximized":null}` in file â†’ Load â†’ strips both fields â†’ next Write â†’ adds them again. The byte-identical claim is false and misleads maintainers about the persistence format.
recommendation: Fix `applyViewState` to omit fields when empty: only write `minimized` when the array is non-empty (`minimized?.length ? { minimized } : {}`), and only write `maximized` when non-null. Alternatively, remove the byte-identical claim from the `normalizeViewState` comment and document the actual round-trip behavior.
resolution: resolved (TASK-020): applyViewState omits empty view-state on write.

claim: The `useMemo` computing `visible` uses `[ws.layout, minimizedIds]` as its dependency array but suppresses `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` via an inline `// eslint-disable-line` comment. The suppression is correct today â€” `computeVisibleLayout({ ...ws, minimized: minimizedIds })` only reads `ws.layout` from `ws` â€” but it makes future regressions invisible. If `computeVisibleLayout` is extended to read additional workspace fields (e.g., `ws.panes` for validation), the memo will silently return stale results with no lint warning.
recommendation: Eliminate the need for the suppression by passing only the fields that matter to the memo computation: either pass `ws.layout` directly and construct the minimal argument inline (`computeVisibleLayout({ layout: ws.layout, panes: ws.panes, minimized: minimizedIds, ... })`), listing each used field in the dep array; or refactor `computeVisibleLayout` to accept `(layout, minimizedIds)` as separate arguments. Either approach lets the linter police future changes without a blanket suppression.
resolution: resolved (TASK-020): eslint-disable removed in WorkspaceView memo.

claim: `chipStatus`'s entire body is `return { state, needsInput: state === 'needs-input', recording, ai }` â€” it derives one boolean from `state` and passes the other two fields through unchanged. The function encodes no meaningful logic; its sole justification is that REQ-005 mandates a pure, unit-testable status-mapping module. Without a comment explaining this, the function looks like under-engineered boilerplate, and future contributors may either inline it (losing testability) or add complexity expecting it to do more. The `ChipStatus` interface is also structurally synonymous with `{ state: TermState; needsInput: boolean; recording: boolean; ai: boolean }`, offering no semantic value beyond the inline shape.
recommendation: Add a brief doc-comment to `chipStatus` (and optionally `ChipStatus`) stating explicitly that the module exists to provide a stable, independently unit-testable surface for REQ-005 and not to encapsulate complex derivation logic. No behavior change required.
resolution: resolved (TASK-020): chipStatus doc-comment added.

claim: `TIP_COMMANDS` (the rotating status-bar discovery list) includes `toggle-maximize-pane` but omits `toggle-minimize-pane`, the symmetric operation added by this feature. The plan marks TIP_COMMANDS inclusion as optional, so this is not a spec violation. However, users who rely on status-bar tips to discover shortcuts will learn about Maximize but not Minimize â€” a discoverability gap for a directly paired command. The asymmetry is especially noticeable since both operations share the same 'Panes' category in `COMMANDS`.
recommendation: Add `'toggle-minimize-pane'` to `TIP_COMMANDS` alongside `'toggle-maximize-pane'`. The tip rotation is round-robin and the list is short, so the addition adds minimal noise while closing the discoverability gap.
resolution: resolved (TASK-020): toggle-minimize-pane added to TIP_COMMANDS.

claim: REQ-012's MUST 'Explorer/editor state MUST be preserved like any kept-mounted pane' (acceptance: 'explorer root/selection preserved') is violated. Minimize is NOT kept-in-place like maximize: it prunes the pane's leaf from the visible mosaic and re-renders the body inside MinimizedPaneHost, so React UNMOUNTS the ExplorerPane in the PaneTile and MOUNTS a fresh instance in the host (and remounts again on restore). ExplorerPane holds all of its view state in LOCAL React state -- `expanded` (which folders are open, line 14), `children` (loaded dir contents, line 13), scroll position -- none of it in `config`, and none of it covered by the transit machinery, which stashes ONLY terminal scrollback (stashSnapshot) and editor drafts (beginPaneTransit). On remount the useEffect at line 69 resets `expanded` to `new Set([config.root])`, collapsing every folder the user had open. So minimizing an explorer with subfolders expanded restores it fully collapsed -- explorer state is lost, contradicting the 'preserved like any kept-mounted pane' contract (a maximized/hidden explorer, which is genuinely kept-mounted in place, does NOT lose this). TEST-030 cannot catch it: it only asserts the always-present top-level entry 'entry-note.ts' reappears (which survives a full reset) and never expands a subfolder before minimizing. The requirement is pinned by a shallow happy-path test.
recommendation: Either (a) extend the minimize/restore transit with an explorer view-state branch (persist expanded set / scroll across the remount, sibling of the terminal snapshot / editor draft), or (b) lift durable explorer view-state (expanded folders) into the persisted PaneConfig so a remount rehydrates it, or (c) narrow REQ-012 to guarantee only config-level state and explicitly accept expanded-tree reset. Strengthen TEST-030 to expand a subfolder, minimize, restore, and assert it is still expanded.
resolution: re-review CONFIRMED-CLOSED: explorer-registry stash bridges expanded/scroll across remount (TASK-012); TEST-038 green.

claim: The off-layout host sizes EVERY minimized pane to `inset: 0` (full workspace body). The code comment justifies this as avoiding a PTY resize, but that reasoning only holds for the single-pane case it names ('a pane that occupied the whole body before minimize'). For any multi-tile workspace, a pane that was e.g. a 50%-width tile is remounted at 100% width in the host -- a genuine cols/rows change, i.e. a real ConPTY repaint on minimize -- and the right-split restore (REQ-006) changes the size again, a second repaint. The REQ-003/REQ-005 e2es (TEST-026, TEST-033) only exercise the single-pane geometry, so this double-resize-on-reflow path -- including the documented status-tail repaint-eviction risk -- is never tested for a backgrounded multi-tile terminal, which is exactly where REQ-005 promises to surface needs-input on the chip.
recommendation: Size the off-layout host to the pane's pre-minimize (or eventual restored) tile geometry so minimize/restore does not force an avoidable ConPTY repaint, and add a multi-tile variant of TEST-026/TEST-033 that minimizes one of >=2 panes and asserts output still accumulates / needs-input still marks the chip.
resolution: resolved: MinimizedPaneHost sizes to pre-minimize tile geometry (TASK-018); TEST-037 green.

claim: REQ-005 enumerates the chip's live states as running/idle/needs-input/recording/AI but omits process-exited. A minimized terminal is NOT auto-closed when its shell exits (TerminalPane only writes '[process exited]' to the now-hidden xterm at line 84), so its tray chip keeps rendering data-status from the last statuses[paneId] (typically 'idle'), with the default icon and 'Restore <name> (idle)' label, indefinitely. A dead terminal is visually indistinguishable from a live idle one -- the opposite of REQ-005's stated goal that a backgrounded pane's true state never becomes a silent footgun, and worse than the visible case where the user at least sees the '[process exited]' line. This is a missing acceptance criterion in REQ-005, not just an implementation slip.
recommendation: Add an 'exited' state to chipStatus and surface it on the chip with a distinct icon/label (or auto-prune a minimized chip whose pane process has exited). Add a test that exits a minimized terminal and asserts the chip reflects exited rather than idle.
resolution: resolved: exited chip state added (TASK-019); TEST-040 green.

claim: Undock relies on the destination window LOADING the workspace record from disk (applyAssignment -> api.loadWorkspace), but the move/undock handoff never requests a workspace-record flush -- it only captures terminal scrollback snapshots (captureSnapshots). The minimized/maximized view-state reaches disk only via the 500ms debounced autosave (AUTOSAVE_DEBOUNCE_MS, scheduled by toggleMinimize). So a minimize-then-undock within the debounce window hands a STALE (un-minimized) record to the new window, dropping the minimized state -- exactly the corruption REQ-015 forbids ('undocking a workspace that has a minimized pane preserves the minimized state in the new window'). TEST-035 only passes because a human drag plus the 500ms snapshot timeout normally exceed the autosave debounce; the correctness of a cross-window handoff is left riding a timer, not a flush. propose CONV: a cross-window/process handoff that re-reads persisted state from disk MUST flush the relevant pending debounced save before the reader loads it, never relying on the debounce timer.
recommendation: Flush the source window's pending workspace autosave (or serialize the in-memory view-state into the handoff) before the destination window loads the record from disk, so the handoff does not depend on debounce timing. Add a test that minimizes then undocks immediately and asserts the minimized state survives.
resolution: resolved: serializeWorkspace flushes pending autosave before undock handoff (TASK-009 ext); TEST-035 green.

claim: `toggleMaximize` does not check whether the target pane is currently minimized before writing `maximized[wsId] = paneId`. User-triggerable via normal UI: minimize the focused pane (toggleMinimize never clears focusedPaneId), then press toggle-maximize-pane â€” App.tsx passes the stale focusedPaneId to toggleMaximize, which accepts it because the pane still exists in ws.panes. Result: a pane simultaneously in minimized[wsId] AND maximized[wsId]. applyViewState persists both, so the incoherent state survives reload and becomes on-disk ground truth. Violates REQ-010 (maximize and minimize MUST be mutually exclusive for the same pane). The minimize path enforces this (minimizePane clears maximized) but the maximize path does not enforce the reverse.
recommendation: Add `if ((s.minimized[wsId] ?? []).includes(paneId)) return` (or clear it from minimized) at the top of the toggleMaximize setter; refocus a visible pane at the end of toggleMinimize to close the stale-focus window.
resolution: re-review CONFIRMED-CLOSED: toggleMaximize no-ops a minimized pane (TASK-013); TEST-041 green.

claim: applyViewState always writes minimized:[] and maximized:null unconditionally. The load path (normalizeViewState) documents/implements the invariant that absent fields represent empty state for byte-identical round-trips, but the save path contradicts this every cycle. No data loss or injection risk (normalizeViewState self-heals on reload), but the write/read asymmetry is a defense-in-depth gap for any future integrity-checking layer.
recommendation: Omit empty view-state fields on write (only write minimized when non-empty, maximized when non-null), or drop the byte-identical claim from the comment.
resolution: resolved (TASK-020): applyViewState omit-empty fixes the write/read asymmetry.

claim: The .min-tray container has pointer-events:auto on a near-full-width, z-index:6 transparent box pinned to the bottom of the workspace body. A transparent element with pointer-events:auto still eats clicks, so whenever >=1 pane is minimized the entire bottom strip silently swallows mouse clicks (even between chips), overlaying the prompt/last rows of the reflowed terminal. Hard-to-diagnose regression to the core terminal flow; untested because REQ-002 geometry assertion only checks tile width, not the overlay.
recommendation: Set container pointer-events:none and chips pointer-events:auto (or reserve a real layout strip so the tray does not overlap the reflowed terminal).
resolution: re-review CONFIRMED-CLOSED: .min-tray pointer-events:none + chips auto (TASK-016); TEST-043 green.

claim: Minimizing the focused pane via the keybinding orphans keyboard focus: the body goes visibility:hidden and nothing refocuses a surviving visible pane, so typing goes to <body> until the user mouses to another pane. restorePane focuses; toggleMinimize does not. Spec (REQ-001) never specified the post-minimize focus target.
recommendation: After minimizing the focused pane, move focus to a sensible surviving visible pane (or the tray chip). Mirror the focus handling restorePane already does.
resolution: resolved: toggleMinimize refocuses a surviving visible pane (TASK-013).

claim: The context-menu Minimize item â€” one of REQ-001 three mandated entry points â€” lives in the portalled pane-menu, which is absent from the focus-visible AND hover allow-lists (unlike ws-menu/proc-menu/cwd-menu/split-menu and the new tray chips). It carries no themed accent focus ring and zero hover affordance, violating REQ-014 / CONV-007 (any tray/menu chrome portalled to <body> MUST carry paint-only focus and hover styling, or be added to the allow-lists).
recommendation: Add [data-testid="pane-menu"] to the hover/active/focus-visible style groups in index.css so the portalled menu items show focus and hover.
resolution: re-review CONFIRMED-CLOSED: pane-menu in hover/active/disabled/focus-visible allow-lists (TASK-015); TEST-042 green.

claim: decisions.md still calls maximize a transient maximized[wsId] flag and asserts it is never serialized (serializeWorkspace only writes {id,name,layout,panes,theme}). Both now contradict the persisted code (REQ-008). Missed because REQ-019 hand-listed the docs to reconcile and omitted decisions.md, and TEST-023 guard only matches one literal phrase in CLAUDE.md so decisions.md wording slips through.
recommendation: Update decisions.md to reflect persisted maximize+minimize view-state and the v6->v7 schema. Consider a CONV to grep the whole docs/ tree when retiring a stale claim.
resolution: resolved (doc-sync): verified decisions.md no longer calls maximize transient/never-serialized — the 2026-06 minimize/restore update block (l.484-493) plus the amended Consequences (l.504-506) record persisted minimize+maximize view-state and the v6->v7 schema. A grep of the whole docs/ tree surfaced one remaining historical mention in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-17-pane-titlebar-actions-design.md (a dated point-in-time design artifact); annotated with a superseded-by pointer rather than rewritten. Lesson promoted to CONV-008.

claim: The Key files table and Testing section are not updated for MinimizedTray.tsx, MinimizedPaneHost.tsx, chip-status.ts, and the new minimize unit/e2e specs (the narrative section is fine).
recommendation: Add the new components/modules and test files to the Key files table and Testing section in workspaces.md.
resolution: resolved (doc-sync, originally TASK-010): docs/features/workspaces.md Key-files table (l.41-48) now lists MinimizedPaneHost.tsx, MinimizedTray.tsx, explorer-registry.ts, pane-geometry.ts, and chip-status.ts; the Testing section (l.181,185) lists the new unit specs (minimize-persistence/minimize-view-state/chip-status/minimize-keybinding/minimize-pane-menu-style/docs-feature-0003) and e2e specs (minimize-restore/uniform/persistence/tray-status/undock/hardening). Verified complete.

claim: Same-window minimize/restore stashes a terminal's current renderer snapshot before the React layout update, then unmounts the source TerminalPane and mounts a new hidden/visible TerminalPane later. During that gap TerminalPane has removed its onPtyData subscription and the preload pushChannel only fans out to currently registered subscribers; unlike the multi-window handoff, no main-side transit buffer is armed. PTY output emitted after the stash but before the new listener registers can be dropped from renderer scrollback, violating REQ-003/C2's output-accumulates/kept-live guarantee.
recommendation: Use the same buffered handoff machinery for same-window minimize/restore, or keep a stable per-pane renderer data sink mounted across the transition so pty:data is buffered until the destination TerminalPane attaches. Add an e2e/unit harness that emits output during the transition window and asserts it appears after restore.
resolution: re-review CONFIRMED-CLOSED: same-window buffered transit armed (TASK-011); TEST-036 green. Residual sub-ms arming gap tracked separately as DA-005 (non-blocking).

claim: normalizeViewState prunes dangling minimized/maximized pane ids independently but does not enforce REQ-010 mutual exclusion when both fields reference the same valid pane. A persisted workspace with minimized:['p1'] and maximized:'p1' loads with both states intact, so reload can preserve an incoherent same-pane minimized+maximized state instead of normalizing it away.
recommendation: During normalizeViewState, if maximized is also present in minimized, clear maximized or drop that pane from minimized according to the chosen REQ-010 precedence. Add a persistence test for a current-version workspace file containing the same pane in both fields.
resolution: re-review CONFIRMED-CLOSED: normalizeViewState minimize-wins on load (TASK-014); TEST-039 green.

claim: New pty:transit-begin IPC handler discards the sender and passes the renderer-supplied paneId to beginSameWindowTransit with NO ownership validation (unlike every other ownership-sensitive handler which validates e.sender via windowIdOf). (1) Cross-window output withholding: Window A can arm a transit buffer for Window B pane, making it unresponsive up to TRANSIT_GC_MS(10s) per arm. (2) Unbounded buffer growth / memory DoS: beginSameWindowTransit clears the GC timer but does not flush the existing buffer before re-arming, so repeated arms < 10s apart accumulate pty:data without bound. Violates C5 single-owner / main-owns-windows.
recommendation: Validate ownership in the handler: wm.paneOwner.get(id)===wm.windowIdOf(e.sender) before delegating (mirror claimPane/winReady windowIdOf guard); no-op if sender unknown or pane unowned. Add a buffer cap (flush via replayInto when buffer exceeds e.g. 1024 events / ~4MB). Optionally debounce re-arms on the same paneId.
resolution: resolved: beginSameWindowTransit resolves windowIdOf(sender) and no-ops on unknown/unowned/foreign pane (mirrors claimPane, C5); re-arm flushes pending buffer via replayInto; routeToPane caps at TRANSIT_BUFFER_MAX=1024. Verified in window-manager.ts:254-256,210 + register-pty.ts:77 + register.ts:42. Unit 689/689, e2e TEST-036/037 green.

claim: clearTileSize is exported but never called; PaneTile ResizeObserver cleanup disconnects the observer but never clears the sizes map entry, so closed panes leak entries (bounded, UUIDs never reused, zero correctness impact) and the teardown contract modeled by sibling registries is broken.
recommendation: Call clearTileSize(paneId) in PaneTile useEffect cleanup, or remove the unused export.
resolution: resolved: clearTileSize(paneId) added to PaneTile ResizeObserver cleanup.

claim: Residual snapshot-vs-arm gap in the CODEX-001 fix: the terminal snapshot is read synchronously right after the fire-and-forget ptyTransitBegin send, but main begins buffering only when it PROCESSES that message (one IPC latency later); pty:data routed in that sub-ms window reaches only the soon-disposed old xterm, landing in neither the snapshot nor the buffer. Same inherited limitation as the accepted multi-window/cross-workspace handoff (snapshot-then-async-arm), so CODEX-001 is narrowed not fully eliminated. Only manifests under sustained high-frequency output.
recommendation: Capture the snapshot at the cut point that aligns with the arm (read in old TerminalPane unmount cleanup, or make ptyTransitBegin a sendSync handshake returning once armed, de-duping the overlap with a sequence marker). Add a high-frequency-contiguous-output stress test. NOTE: accepted architectural parity with existing handoff; tracked as a known limitation / follow-up.
resolution: DEFERRED (doc-sync) as an accepted known limitation — NOT fixed in code. The residual sub-millisecond snapshot-vs-arm window has architectural parity with the already-accepted multi-window/cross-workspace handoff (snapshot-then-async-arm), so CODEX-001 is narrowed, not eliminated; it only manifests under sustained high-frequency contiguous output. Recorded as a deferred follow-up in docs/superpowers/0003-pane-minimize-restore-review-followups.md with the recommended cut-point-handshake fix (capture the snapshot at the arm cut point, or make ptyTransitBegin a sendSync handshake returning once armed, deduped by a sequence marker) and a high-frequency-output stress-test obligation.
